bugsFound.title: generatePdfFromCloudRun is not defined anywhere
bugsFound.description: Function generatePdfFromCloudRun() is called in requests.js:2956 and firebaseService.js:14,104 but was never defined. Causes ReferenceError at runtime.
bugsFound.impact: New request submission (processAndSignDocument) always fails with 'cannot create document' error. Users cannot submit new requests using the main flow.
bugsFound.fix: Replaced all calls with generateOfficialPDF() which is defined in admin.js and handles 'memo' and 'command' doctypes correctly. Set formData.doctype='memo' and formData.btnId appropriately before calling.

bugsFound.title: blobToBase64 defined in 3 files — admin.js version is unsafe
bugsFound.description: blobToBase64() is defined in utils.js (safe, null-checks result), firebaseService.js (safe), and admin.js (unsafe — reader.result.split(',')[1] can throw TypeError if result is null). Since admin.js loads last, the unsafe version overrides the safe ones globally.
bugsFound.impact: If FileReader fails for any reason, admin.js version throws unhandled TypeError instead of returning empty string.
bugsFound.fix: Removed duplicates from firebaseService.js and admin.js. Only the safe version in utils.js remains.

bugsFound.title: IS_DEBUG = true hardcoded in production code
bugsFound.description: config.js:4 had IS_DEBUG=true which kept all console.log/warn/error/info active everywhere.
bugsFound.impact: Performance overhead, potential information disclosure via console in production.
bugsFound.fix: Changed to auto-detect: IS_DEBUG = true only when hostname is localhost/127.0.0.1/''. Automatically off on production domain.

bugsFound.title: Firebase API keys exposed in client-side code
bugsFound.description: Firebase project credentials (apiKey, appId, messagingSenderId, etc.) are hardcoded in config.js and visible to any user who views source.
bugsFound.impact: Risk of quota abuse or unauthorized Firebase access if Firestore security rules are permissive.
bugsFound.recommendation: This is acceptable for Firebase client-side SDKs ONLY IF Firestore Security Rules are properly configured to restrict read/write access by authenticated users. Verify and harden security rules.

bugsFound.title: Google Apps Script URL exposed in client-side code
bugsFound.description: The GAS endpoint URL is fully visible in config.js:52. Anyone can call it with arbitrary payloads.
bugsFound.impact: Potential unauthorized data access or modification if GAS does not validate user identity/role.
bugsFound.recommendation: Add server-side authentication in GAS: verify a signed token or session cookie before processing any action.

bugsFound.title: Session storage used for sensitive user data
bugsFound.description: getCurrentUser() (utils.js:135) reads user object from sessionStorage including role and username. XSS attacks could read this data.
bugsFound.impact: User role and identity could be stolen via XSS, enabling privilege escalation if security is client-side only.
bugsFound.recommendation: Ensure all server-side operations (GAS, Firestore rules) independently verify user identity. Do not rely solely on client-side role from sessionStorage.

bugsFound.title: Password padding for Firebase — no UI enforcement of minimum length
bugsFound.description: auth.js pads passwords shorter than 6 characters with '0's for Firebase but the UI had no minimum length check, allowing users to create weak passwords without warning.
bugsFound.impact: Users with short passwords could create accounts without understanding the padding behavior.
bugsFound.fix: Added explicit minimum length check (>= 6 characters) in both handleRegister() and handlePasswordUpdate(). Shows clear Thai-language error via showAlert before the Firebase padding logic is reached.

bugsFound.title: No CSRF protection on GAS API calls
bugsFound.description: apiCall() in utils.js sends POST requests with JSON body and Content-Type: text/plain. GAS does not validate request origin.
bugsFound.impact: Cross-site request forgery possible if attacker can get authenticated users to load malicious pages.
bugsFound.recommendation: Add a CSRF token or Origin header validation in GAS doPost() handler.

bugsFound.title: Approval token links have no expiry warning
bugsFound.description: tokenSign.js creates 7-day approval links, but there was no in-UI warning when the link is about to expire.
bugsFound.impact: Approvers may miss deadlines; links expire silently.
bugsFound.fix: Added in-page amber warning banner when token expires in less than 24 hours, showing exact hours remaining. Full email notification still requires GAS-side implementation.
bugsFound.remainingWork: Server-side email notification via GAS when link is created or near expiry.

bugsFound.title: Cache not cleared after admin delete/status-update operations
bugsFound.description: deleteRequestByAdmin() and deleteMemoByAdmin() did not clear user-side request cache after deletion. Status update functions did not clear window.userRequestsCache after changing request status.
bugsFound.impact: Users could see deleted records or old status values for up to 5 minutes after an admin action.
bugsFound.fix: Added clearRequestsCache() in delete functions. Added targeted window.userRequestsCache=null in status update functions (without clearing allRequestsCache which admin needs immediately).

bugsFound.title: validateRequestForm used bare alert() and showed double-error on form submit
bugsFound.description: validateRequestForm() used alert() (not the styled showAlert modal) and showed generic field labels. When called from handleRequestFormSubmit(), a second generic error alert was also shown from the catch block.
bugsFound.impact: Inconsistent UI — raw browser alert() instead of styled modal; users saw two error messages on validation failure.
bugsFound.fix: Rewrote validateRequestForm to use showAlert with specific Thai field-level messages per missing field. Changed handleRequestFormSubmit to return early (not throw) after validation failure, preventing double-alert.

codeQualityNotes.note: Hybrid Firebase+GAS design is intentional and works. GAS is authoritative for IDs and file storage; Firebase is for real-time UI updates.
Most async operations have try/catch with user-friendly alerts and console logging. Cloud Run failures fall back to GAS gracefully.
admin.js (2526 lines) and requests.js (3176 lines) are very large. Consider splitting into smaller modules if the project grows.
No bundler (webpack/vite). All JS is loaded as separate CDN/local scripts. No TypeScript, no unit tests.
THSarabunNew fonts are loaded locally. PDF generation will fail if fonts are missing or CORS-blocked.
CDN libraries use fixed versions (e.g., Chart.js 3.7.1) — good. Tailwind CSS uses latest CDN — could break on major version.

performanceNotes.detail: Promise.all() used in several admin views
Triple fallback: Firestore cache → GAS proxy → direct fetch
11 JS files loaded separately; no minification
template_memo.docx is 3.4 MB — slow on mobile
pdf-lib merge in browser can be slow for large files

changes.description: Replaced undefined generatePdfFromCloudRun() call with generateOfficialPDF({...formData, doctype:'memo', btnId:'submit-button'})
Replaced generatePdfFromCloudRun('template_memo.docx', formData) with generateOfficialPDF({...formData, doctype:'memo', btnId:null})
Replaced generatePdfFromCloudRun(templateName, data) with generateOfficialPDF({...data, doctype:'command', templateType:..., btnId:null})
Removed unsafe duplicate blobToBase64() definition (was missing null check on reader.result)

openRecommendations: Verify and harden Firestore Security Rules to enforce role-based access (BUG-004)
Add authentication/authorization check in Google Apps Script doPost() handler (BUG-005)
Ensure all server-side (GAS) actions verify user role independently of client (BUG-006)
Pin Tailwind CSS CDN to a specific version to prevent unexpected breakage
Add server-side email notification via GAS when approval token links are created/near expiry (BUG-009 remaining)
Consider Firestore onSnapshot() real-time listeners for admin views to replace polling cache

fixLog.by: Claude Code Audit — Initial Bug Fixes

changes.description: BUG-001: Replaced undefined generatePdfFromCloudRun() with generateOfficialPDF({doctype:'memo', btnId:'submit-button'})
BUG-001: Replaced both generatePdfFromCloudRun() calls with generateOfficialPDF()
BUG-002: Removed unsafe duplicate blobToBase64() (missing null-check on reader.result)

fixLog.by: Claude Code Audit — Full Bug Fix Pass

changes.description: BUG-003: IS_DEBUG now auto-detects localhost vs production hostname instead of hardcoded true
BUG-007: Added minimum 6-character password validation in handleRegister() and handlePasswordUpdate() before Firebase padding logic
BUG-009: Added amber warning banner in handleTokenSignFlow() when token expires in less than 24 hours
BUG-010: Added clearRequestsCache() in deleteRequestByAdmin() and deleteMemoByAdmin(). Added targeted window.userRequestsCache=null in status update functions.
BUG-011: Rewrote validateRequestForm() to use showAlert with specific Thai field-level messages. Fixed double-alert by returning early in handleRequestFormSubmit instead of throwing.
